channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Babu Owino Promises Major City Hall Changes and Criticises Key Appointments Ahead of 2027 Race
item.description: Embakasi East MP Babu Owino has declared that he plans to implement major changes at Nairobi City Hall if he wins the gubernatorial seat in the 2027 elections. Speaking during an interview on NTV Keny...

item.title: SAPS Captain Recognised for Courage During Crocodile Recovery Operation
item.description: In an extraordinary display of bravery, Captain Johan 'Pottie' Potgieter of the South African Police Service (SAPS) was commended for his courageous actions during a challenging operation along the Ko...

item.title: Beyoncé Returns to the Met Gala with Family After a Decade
item.description: Beyoncé returned to the Met Gala for the first time in ten years, making a memorable red carpet appearance alongside her daughter Blue Ivy Carter and husband Jay-Z. The pop superstar wore a striking B...

item.title: Vijay Fan Club Leaders Secure Key Wins in Tamil Nadu Elections
item.description: In a remarkable turn of events in the 2026 Tamil Nadu assembly elections, K V Vijay Damu, a former fan club leader, won the Royapuram seat representing the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK). Prior to his...

item.title: Fervo Energy Plans $1.3 Billion IPO to Expand Geothermal Energy Projects
item.description: Fervo Energy, a U.S.-based geothermal energy startup, announced its plans to raise up to $1.3 billion in an initial public offering (IPO). If the shares sell at the upper end of its $21 to $24 target ...

item.title: High Court Bans Graffiti and Tinted Windows on Matatus, Raising Economic and Cultural Concerns
item.description: The High Court has upheld the NTSA directive banning graffiti, decorative art, and tinted windows on Kenyan matatus, citing road safety as the main concern. Matatus are not just public transport; they...

item.title: Alberta separatist group submits over 300,000 signatures for potential referendum
item.description: The Stay Free Alberta group has submitted 301,620 signatures calling for a referendum on Alberta's potential separation from Canada, surpassing the 178,000 threshold needed to prompt consideration by ...

item.title: Delhi High Court dismisses SpiceJet's review petition to defer Rs 144 crore deposit
item.description: The Delhi High Court on Monday rejected the review petition filed by SpiceJet and its promoter, Ajay Singh, seeking a reconsideration of an earlier order to deposit Rs 144 crore. The airline had cited...

item.title: PCEA Pastor Shot Dead in Church Compound in Lanet, Nakuru
item.description: In Nakuru County, a pastor serving at PCEA Tabuga in Lanet, Bahati Constituency, was tragically killed by armed suspects who broke into his residence within the church compound on the night of April 3...

item.title: UK Police Push for Christian Brueckner to Face Trial Over Madeleine McCann Case
item.description: Detectives from the Metropolitan Police are seeking to bring Christian Brueckner to trial in the United Kingdom for the abduction and murder of Madeleine McCann, who disappeared in Portugal in May 200...

item.title: RBA Likely to Hike Interest Rates Following Inflation Increase
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) is set to announce an interest rate increase this afternoon, with economists from ten major banks largely in agreement. This decision comes shortly after the releas...

item.title: Elon Musk settles SEC lawsuit over delayed Twitter stake disclosure with $1.5M penalty
item.description: Elon Musk has reached a settlement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regarding a civil lawsuit concerning the timing of his disclosure of Twitter, now known as X, stock purchases....

item.title: MIT researchers map the atomic structure of relaxor ferroelectrics
item.description: Researchers at MIT have for the first time mapped the three-dimensional atomic structure of relaxor ferroelectrics, a type of material critical for technologies like ultrasound imaging, microphones, a...

item.title: NDMA issues safety guidelines as Pakistan faces record heatwave
item.description: RAWALPINDI – The National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA) has issued comprehensive emergency protocols across Pakistan in response to an unusually severe heatwave expected from May to June. Autho...

item.title: Classroom Disorder Linked to Higher Bullying Rates in Elementary Schools
item.description: Bullying affects roughly one in four elementary school students in the United States each year and can have long-term consequences, including academic struggles, mental health issues, and increased ri...

item.title: Bridget Fonda's Journey to Health and Life Away from Hollywood
item.description: Bridget Fonda, the retired Hollywood actress, has amazed many with her impressive weight loss of roughly 75-100 lbs since stepping away from the cameras. Fonda, who left Hollywood in 2002 to focus on ...

item.title: Barack Obama discusses marital tensions linked to political involvement
item.description: In a candid interview with The New Yorker, former US President Barack Obama opened up about the strains in his marriage caused by his continued engagement in politics. Michelle Obama reportedly wishes...

item.title: Kenyan Care Worker in UK Faces Long Wait for Settlement Due to Policy Changes
item.description: Lydia Kabute, a 41-year-old Kenyan mother of two, moved to the UK in 2023 to help fill the care worker shortage. Initially, it looked like a chance to build a better future while supporting vulnerable...

item.title: Man City Draw Everton 3-3 as Doku Scores Late Equalizer
item.description: For Monday EPL match wey happen for Hill Dickinson stadium, Manchester City comot last-minute equalizer against Everton, end the game 3-3. Jeremy Doku show im class by scoring two fine goals, includin...

item.title: Light Rail Extension to Woden Faces Engineering and Economic Challenges
item.description: The announcement of the proposed light rail extension from Civic to Woden is a positive step but far from the final stage. While the plan is supported by pragmatists who acknowledge the substantial su...
